reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.title: Best Sleep-Aid Investment Ever
reviews.content_html:
I’ve tried a handful of sleep aids over the years, and the Hatch Restore 3 is the first one that’s actually made a noticeable difference in both how I fall asleep and how I wake up.

The setup was quick and intuitive, and the companion app is clean and easy to navigate. What really stands out is how customizable everything is, you can build a full nighttime routine with different sounds, lights, and wind-down cues that signal your brain it’s time to relax. The sound quality is surprisingly rich for a device this size, and the library of sleep sounds and meditations is extensive without feeling overwhelming.

The sunrise alarm feature is a game changer. Instead of being jolted awake by a harsh alarm, the gradual light makes waking up feel much more natural and less stressful. I’ve noticed I’m getting out of bed more easily and feeling less groggy in the mornings.

Design-wise, it looks great on a nightstand—minimalist and modern without being distracting. The physical controls are also a nice touch if you don’t want to reach for your phone at night.

It’s not the cheapest option out there, but in my opinion, it’s absolutely worth the investment if you’re serious about improving your sleep routine. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Eoin O
reviews.title: Very Satisfied with my Hatch 3
reviews.content_html:
I chose the Cocoa color and was happy to find that the color matched my nightstand. I’ve been able to use the sleep and rise features for the last week and it has improved my quality of sleep. I would recommend this product to someone looking for a premium way of improving their daily life. If you own premium bedding, pillows, and lighting, the Hatch 3 is a no-brainer addition.

The Good:
The product has a very nice surface feel and weight. The buttons and sliders have a nice tactile feel and I find myself fidgeting with them sometimes. It’s a pretty decent size comparable to most bedside alarm clocks.
Its lights are controllable and can be very bright. Its speaker is also controllable and can get very loud if needed. I am a heavy sleeper and the “birds” wake up noise had no problem getting me awake.
It has a wide variety of colors and prebuilt music, white noise, alarms and short stories. This is all done through the app.

What I wish it had also:
While the Hatch 3 runs through the app, I would’ve liked ways to play my own music or use it easier without my phone. You can use the buttons to change between premade combinations but sometimes I would’ve liked to use it as a bedside speaker or nightlight without using the app.

Overall I am happy with my purchase. It is well made and improves my quality of life. Attached are photos of the hatch in bedside mode, color mode, lights and noise at night, the box, a rough size comparison to my hands(I have large hands) and some screenshots of the app.

reviews.author: Megan
reviews.title: Game changer for early morning wake ups
reviews.content_html:
I’ve only used the Restore 3 for one night, but I’m already impressed. I’ve honestly never woken up this easily before. I have never been a morning person and typically struggle getting out of bed in the morning. However, despite having to wake up earlier than usual this morning, my body woke up naturally with the gradual sunrise. It’s also nice being able to wake up without the harsh iPhone alarm. This morning, I was actually awake before the sound even went off.

I’m still getting to know all the features, but so far I absolutely love it and can already tell it’s going to be a game changer for my mornings (especially during the darker months). Setup was easy and the app is very user friendly.

Also love how aesthetic it looks on my nightstand and that I can easily control it from the device, without having to look at my phone in the morning. Excited to continue to explore all the features available!

reviews.author: Nicole
reviews.title: All-in-one sleep support
reviews.content_html:
I’ve been using this for a little while now and it has become one of my favorite parts of my nightly routine.

First, the design is beautiful. It’s sleek, minimal, and looks great in my room without taking up much space. When it’s off, it blends in nicely, but when it’s on, the lighting adds a fun pop of color while still feeling calm.

What I appreciate most is how versatile it is. I’ve slept with pink noise for years, but this lets me build a more thoughtful routine around that. I can start with a sound bath, fall asleep with pink noise, and wake up to something more playful. The combination of sound and lighting makes it feel really personalized.

It also makes me feel like I’m taking better care of myself and my sleep without much effort. Everything feels simple and intentional, which has made bedtime feel a little more enjoyable.

Setup was very easy and straightforward, and I had everything running quickly.

Overall, I’m really happy with it and excited to see how the app continues to improve over time.

reviews.author: Paul Forrise
reviews.title: Two AuDHD adults, two Restore 3s, zero regrets
reviews.content_html:
If you've ever laid in bed staring at the ceiling while a smart speaker cut out mid-rainstorm, or fought a $25 Amazon sound machine with mediocre sound quality that you had to manually start and stop every single night, this review is for you.

My wife and I both have AuDHD, and sound at night isn't a preference, it's the difference between a night of sleep and a night of our brains refusing to shut up. Over the years we've run the gauntlet: Alexa with scheduled rain routines, a Google Home doing the same thing, and eventually a cheap $20–$30 sound machine off Amazon when we got tired of the smart speakers dropping audio every time the internet hiccuped. Each one had a fatal flaw. The smart speakers couldn't be trusted to actually play through the night. The cheap sound machine was reliable but had to be manually started and stopped every single night, and the sound quality was, being generous, about on par with a Google Home speaker.

Then I bought a Hatch Restore 3 in late 2024, and the story changed.

I used mine solo for about a year while my wife stuck with the cheap Amazon unit. This was her choice. She didn't think that the Hatch would provide much difference in sleep quality. Then one night she slept on my side of the bed. The next morning, she all but demanded I get her one of her own. The sound quality had put her into a depth of sleep she hadn't hit in years. We now have two Restore 3s on the nightstands, both in Cocoa.

What actually makes it work:

Sound: We both use Green Noise as our sleep sound, and it does something specific for an AuDHD brain that rain and white noise never quite did. It shuts down the overthinking center and lulls you down. The speaker is genuinely high quality, not a rebadged cheap driver, and because the core sounds are stored on the device itself, a flaky internet connection doesn't cut playback the way it did with our Google Homes. It just plays.

The rise routine: This is where the Hatch earns its price tag. My current setup is a ten-minute sunrise on the Portland Lighthouse light preset, with Fisherman's Wharf (seagulls, water) as the wake audio. It ramps gently instead of detonating like an iPhone alarm. I rotate the light and sound combinations based on mood, which keeps it from going stale.

Hold-to-dismiss + the light: This is the feature that changed my wife's mornings. Her pattern used to be: hear phone alarm, roll over, slap dismiss, fall back asleep with zero memory of having done it. With the Hatch, she gets two sensory streams at once, ten minutes of building light plus gentle sound, and by the time she reaches to dismiss, she actually has to hold the button. That extra second of required physical engagement means she's cognitively awake when she turns it off, not reflexively swatting at it.

Look and feel: The Cocoa finish is genuinely nice, soft-touch plastic, understated, doesn't scream "gadget" on the nightstand.

A few honest gripes:

The alarm and schedule setup lives entirely in the app. The device has physical buttons for volume, brightness, pause/advance, and a quick alarm disable, but building or editing a routine requires your phone. If you're shopping for a simple, self-contained clock, this is the wrong product. For anyone already comfortable with app-managed devices, it's a non-issue, but it's worth calling out.

My bigger wish is device linking. With two units in the same room, each alarm is managed independently. When one of us dismisses ours, the other keeps going. It's manageable, but I'd pay for a "paired bedroom" mode tomorrow. (Possible I'm missing it in settings, but I've looked.)

We also had the six-month Hatch+ trial and used it for a while. The sleep stories and guided meditations are well produced, and I'd recommend them to anyone without an established wind-down routine. For us, we already have our own ritual, a separate galaxy light goes on as part of the lead-in, and we do our own meditations, so Hatch+ didn't stick. The good news is you don't need it. The built-in sounds, Green Noise, Portland Lighthouse, Fisherman's Wharf, the basics, are exactly what we use and they're included.

That's the review. When a device quietly replaces three previous solutions, when your partner sleeps on it once and demands one of her own, when it becomes the thing you trust to actually run the routine every night without babysitting, you stop thinking about it. That's the highest compliment I can give a product at this price point.

2-4. The Alarm settings I described in my review

reviews.author: AML
reviews.title: Best investment!
reviews.content_html: I absolutely love my Hatch!!! The bedtime routines make going to bed so much easier and I love that I can choose between a variety of sounds. Even without the subscription, I feel like there are a lot of great options for free. I am a pretty light sleeper, but the light alone wakes me up in the morning before the sound which is great. I can see this working well for heavy sleepers too since the noise and brightness are gradual and adjustable (you can adjust the timing of how long it takes to reach max brightness and volume too). I’ve told so many of my friends about the alarm clock too. Definitely an investment but I think it’s well worth it since it is used every day!

The Hatch clock is one of the few products that actually earns its place on my nightstand. It looks clean, minimal, and intentional, not like some cheap plasticky gadget screaming for attention. The light is soft and genuinely calming, and the sunrise alarm makes waking up feel way less brutal, especially during dark New York winters. The sound quality is better than I expected too, and the whole experience feels thoughtfully designed from both a visual and usability standpoint. It’s one of those products that quietly improves your daily life without trying too hard.

reviews.author: Scott Crowder
reviews.title: Good device but shouldn’t require subscription
reviews.content_html: The sound machine and sunrise clock work great. But for the price you pay for the clock having to buy a subscription to set it up is outrageous
reviews.content_text: The sound machine and sunrise clock work great. But for the price you pay for the clock having to buy a subscription to set it up is outrageous

reviews.author: Michael X.
reviews.title: Convenient Purchase
reviews.content_html:
My experience has been quite good overall! I've been using the Hatch 3 for a few weeks now as my daily alarm as well a replacement for my bedside lamp, and it has been overall functional.

I've had a few days where I don't wake up to the hatch alarm - I think I'm still adjusting to the ringer as opposed to my iPhone ringer, but nothing too serious as I set backup alarms. I've been enjoying the alarm options from motivational speeches to nature sounds (although those are quite soft). One feature I wish would be implemented would be the option for the light to remain on after turning off the alarm in the morning. Being in the dark again after waking up is too easy for me to fall back asleep.

With a secondary function as a bedside lamp, it provide me with an ample amount of brightness for pre-bedtime doomscrolling. I also enjoy the winddown audios, especially the seaside noises. The ability to change the color of the light depending on my mood is also quite pleasant, although I wish there was a dial on the clock itself to switch the color rather than in the lamp.

I think overall I wish the clock itself had more functionality without needing to use the app. If I had a night routine without my phone, it would be nice to be able to set alarms, adjust brightness, and change lamp colors not via the app, as that would disrupt my winddown.

reviews.author: diara wilson
reviews.title: Pleasantly surprised
reviews.content_html:
I’ve been using the Hatch Restore 3 for a few weeks now, and I can honestly say I’m pleasantly surprised by how much I enjoy it. What I expected to be just a simple alarm clock and sound machine has actually become a really calming and consistent part of my nightly routine. The wind-down features help me disconnect at the end of the day, and the gradual sunrise alarm makes waking up feel much more natural and definitely less jarring than my phone alarm.
My only real drawback is the subscription model. Given the device is already a significant upfront investment, it would be nice if more features were included without an additional ongoing cost. That said, even with that consideration, I’ve still found it to be a worthwhile addition to my routine and something I look forward to using daily.

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.title: There's a subscription. The free version is good enough though, but that's good to know.
reviews.content_html:
First of all, I really like the device itself. However, I wasn't aware of the subscription model when I bought it. I think the device should offer a 1-Month free trial upon purchase so people can see if they even want to use the subscription, and also should offer a larger suite of functions for free users - since the price is already relatively high for an alarm clock.
I've been using it every day and I'm happy with it overall, took me some time to figure out my exact preferences for it, but that was fine and kind of fun. The volume and light get the job done and are nice for relaxing at night before bed and having some ambient sounds to sleep/wake up to instead of a loud blaring alarm.
When I first set it up, the app kept crashing for about 3 days. Not every time, but it was annoying. If that's happening to anyone, I updated the app and restarted my phone and now the app works perfectly!

reviews.author: Jen Yepes
reviews.title: Great alarm, not so great app fluency/customer support
reviews.content_html:
I loved this. It quickly became an essential part of my daily life. I wish I could add steps to the morning routine right after the alarm rings, just so I could listen to a meditation or a podcast after waking up.
That would prevent me from using my phone too soon.

Even though I got the paid app version, sometimes (maybe 4 in 1.5 years) it gets super slow and I need to wait for hours if I want to update any settings.

Something you need to know...customer service is not great. A chat bot is all you are going to get, at least from my experience.

Other than that, I still think is the best alarm clock I could have bought. Extra points for design, size, weight, light, and sound quality.

reviews.author: Verena
reviews.title: Nice
reviews.content_html:
I debated how to rate this. So, for aesthetics and functionality as a sunrise alarm clock 15/10!!!!! It's really a beautiful, soothing, modern looking device that fits perfectly in my space. Occasionally I use a sunset wind down routine while I'm reading. I don't use sounds at night and prefer quiet. So I've never used any of the sleepy time podcasts and all that.

Now for the app- it's just overpriced and overdone, not well designed and keeps a lot of really basic functions behind a paywall. I'm not about to pay 12 dollars a month to have a different species of bird than the one that currently does wake me up at 6 am. It would be nice to have the option though. If you are the kind of person that appreciates curated white noise machines at night, wind down podcasts and so on this could be a great device. For the price I paid for this alarm clock though I think at least basic alarm options should be available without a monthly subscription. Also making the device simply cross compatible with other apps to access my own music and podcasts would be nice.

The reading light is SO bright which is nice. But it's too bright so I never use it. I wish there were an option to customize that. All that being said the basic functionality and beauty the clock and the ease and personalization I can access for my alarm itself is all really high quality.

reviews.author: ssd
reviews.title: They don’t tell you about the subscription until after you bought it …
reviews.content_html:
It’s a fairly expensive alarm clock to begin with but they don’t mention that you also need a monthly subscription as well.
You can get out of it and the clock still runs fairly well without it but the reminders and pop ups are ridiculous… especially considering it’s $160.

Update: I finally broke down and purchased the subscription for $50 a year.
The app / clock runs beautifully with the subscription.
You get a ton of lighting / sounds/ stories etc perks with the membership that you don’t otherwise have access to.
It’s enjoyable and for less than $5 a month, I would say it’s worth it.
I still think they need to be more up front with the total cost of the clock.. before someone purchases it.

reviews.author: Jess Odom
reviews.title: Good Morning Vibes
reviews.content_html: I truly love my Hatch Restore 3. I have always been a multiple snoozer and alarm person. That has not been the case since I got this alarm clock a few weeks ago. I have gotten up multiple days before the sound part of my alarm went off just because the light woke me up so gently. Instead of feeling super sluggish and trying to convince myself to get out of bed I feel truly awake. I do use the wind down features and the ability to use it as a sound machine at night. It is very customizable and I love that. The app can be a little glitchy and not as intuitive as I first thought but I have mostly figured out my way around it. The display on the alarm clock is great and easy to read and you can decide whether you want it on at all times or not. I do feel this was very worth the investment, not just because I can set it through my phone and control so much of it but because my mornings have truly been better since purchasing it. Even on nights when my quality of sleep was terrible waking up with the mock sunrise has made me feel less tired and ready to face the day. Are there other clocks that can do that for less? Probably. But this alarm clock has multiple things going for it and is very well made. If you are like me and don’t mind learning a little new tech and want the capability to have a noise machine/alarm clock/ wind down device you can control with your phone? I say go for it. I am incredibly pleased with the purchase and have enjoyed customizing it and researching different routines to try to see if I can make it even better for myself and my health.

reviews.author: Jessica P.
reviews.title: Alarm clock is great, app needs improvement
reviews.content_html:
I really love the Hatch device itself — the light, sound quality, and routines have already made a noticeable difference in my sleep. The concept is excellent and the hardware delivers.

That said, the app is the weak point. It’s not very intuitive, can be slow/unresponsive, and I’ve experienced syncing issues between the app and the device. Setting up or adjusting routines feels harder and more frustrating than it should be.

If the app experience matched the quality of the product, this would easily be a 5-star device. I hope Hatch continues improving the software, because the hardware is genuinely great.

reviews.author: KC
reviews.title: Limited Sound Options Without Subscription
reviews.content_html:
PRO: Works as designed…looks nice, offers sound and light functions at scheduled times, brightness and volume.
CONS: Can only be set up when you sign up for premium service through the app. (easily cancelled later)
The sounds offered without the monthly subscription are extremely limited and disappointing.
I’ve found zero for “Cue to Wind Down”, only three for “Sleep and one for “Wake Up”.
I found all other sound offerings counterproductive for my sleep needs.

reviews.author: Sarah Grace
reviews.title: Annoying Alarm Tones
reviews.content_html: I’ll be honest, this was my #1 wedding registry item. I was excited when it finally arrived. Unfortunately, it’s been nothing but a disappointment. Before the hatch, I had one of the OG Samsung sunrise alarm clocks that I loved. The only reason I wanted the hatch was for the different alarm tones because my Samsung one was quite annoying. First of all, I expected an alarm clock with a $55/year subscription to have way more alarm tones options. And honestly, I hated them all. Even Apple has better alarm tones. I also kind of hated that I had to control it from my phone versus just adjusting the alarm time on the alarm itself. One perk that I did enjoy, is that because the whole thing is controlled by your phone, so is the time. So if there’s a power surge in the middle of the night, it automatically synchs back with your phone time and your alarm will still go off at the right time. I have since gone back to my Samsung. I’ll keep the hatch hoping they come out with more alarm tone options that are soft for us light sleepers.

reviews.title: I LIKE it; not LOVE it.
reviews.content_html: I do like this Hatch Alarm Clock and it has a lot of potential for me to actually LOVE it, but I cannot 100% LOVE It. The Hatch's upfront cost itself is already high, only to find that full functionality of The Hatch is paywalled. In other words, I (the customer) have to pay a subscription to be able to lock into The Hatch's full potential. To be quite honest, it is not a very nice tactic to dangle carrots in front of customers that have purchased The Hatch. Also, what I find as a significant source of frustration is discovering that certain light and sound customizations are locked behind The Hatch+ paywall. In sum, The Hatch is quite disappointing regarding the hidden subscription.

reviews.author: Dani
reviews.title: Requires an app and subscription to access most features
reviews.content_html:
First let me say that I am sick to death of subscriptions for practically everything you buy anymore. I definitely don't need nor do I want a subscription for my alarm clock. It's $4.99 a month with only a ONE week free trial period. And, you can't even set it up without signing up for a free trial. They're hoping you forget about the subscription. That is the major reason for the ⭐️⭐️⭐️ star rating. For the price of this clock I would have expected at least 1 year of access to all the content. I canceled the one week free trial within a few days so I wouldn't forget about it and a week isn't enough time to explore the paid content anyway.

As for the clock itself: the app is pretty straightforward and fairly easy to navigate. A lot of the content is behind a pay-wall. There are a lot of sunrise options with various light colors which is nice. The available sound files are okay but not.many choices. The light itself is bright and I need that to overcome sleep inertia. It's not quite as bright as the Lumie 300 which is a little brighter (as documented by the test preformed by the Optimize Your Biology channel and the rigorous testing he did on YouTube) But the Lumie only displays military time and was such a nightmare to program that I gave up on it.

One amusing thing is 2 of my 🐈 🐈⬛ are drawn to it and the 3 of us compete for the spot closest to the light. I guess I'll have to get them one of their own🤪😆 but I think I'll get them one that does not require an app or subscription. Definitely not another one of these. Would I recommend it to a friend? Not likely.

One amusing thing is 2 of my 🐈 🐈⬛ are drawn to it and the 3 of us compete for the spot closest to the light. I guess I'll have to get them one of their own🤪😆 but I think I'll get them one that does not require an app or subscription. Definitely not another one of these. Would I recommend it to a friend? Not likely.

reviews.author: Dan
reviews.title: I was gonna return it…
reviews.content_html:
Three stars is generous - it’s really a two-star review, but I like the white noise feature. Yeah, I know I could have found a cheaper white noise machine. The device itself is pretty cool. The user interface on the iPhone app completely blows. And that’s before we talk about the $49/yr subscription to control the settings via the app. It’s difficult enough to control with the trial version of the app, and became downright obstinate when I canceled the app service within the first thirty days. Honest, I gave serious thought to returning the whole thing because of these difficulties. Let me say it again - the app blows. I’m very technology literate and it’s just a poorly designed app, bad user interface, and not intuitive at all.

For all practical purposes you need a subscription if you want some variety. Fortunately, I’m boring. I’m ok with the same go to bed routine and wake up routine. I’ll be fine with the way I’ve got it set up for the next 20 years. But… I have to manually adjust the volume every night as the app wouldn’t let me save the volume setting. The best part? The white noise settings. I somehow got it to play soft nature settings all night long. It *must* have been a default setting as there’s no way the app was robust enough for me to find that setting. Have I mentioned the app blows? And I’m being kind when I say blows.

I honestly can’t tell the difference between the nature sounds playing all night vs having a window open at a cabin out in the country.

Speaking of deficient users interfaces? The UI on the Restore device itself also blows. I do a long press of the big knob at night before bed and rotate the knob to adjust the volume. A long press of that big knob turns it off in the morning.

The Hatch people market this as an elegant solution - and the overall vibe is cool. The design is a 7 out of ten. The user interface shortcomings drag the score down to an overall rating of 2. The white noise bumps (for me) it back up to a five or six. The lack of a visible clock on the thing is a detraction. The only way I’ve ever gotten the clock to display is to tilt the device. Which is kind of dumb, although I understand the lack of a constant clock was a design decision.

If you’re looking for an alarm clock? Go to Target and spend twenty bucks and get the one with red digits. If you want something that sort of reminds you of an elegant design, and you’re ok with the price and don’t have high expectations about the app, yeah, for sure go get one of these. I’m convinced the white noise is contributing to the eight hours of restful sleep I receive every night.

So, yeah, in limited use cases you’ll like it. If you like to tinker and customize, you’ll probably not like it. Have I mentioned how bad the UI is? Hatch should immediately go out and hire some former Apple design people.

reviews.author: ML
reviews.title: Was I deceived or did I not read the fine print?
reviews.content_html: I like the concept of the Restore and the physical structure. I had the free trial for first 30 days and liked that content however I just could not justify the subscription cost. In inquiring about what was included without the subscription and exploring the library I thought I would be okay. However, I was quickly disappointed. I feel like the product was miss represented with respect to features without a subscription. Had I known what I now know, I would have seriously looked for other options and not paid what I did for this Restore. I could understand maybe $25.00 for a subscription but $60.00 plus is just too much for me to justify on top of the pricey cost of the Restore.

reviews.author: Steven B DeMille
reviews.title: Restless Squirrel Brains - tool to sleep, unwind and wake up gently - recommend!
reviews.content_html: During the final stages of onboarding, the app would keep resetting, so needed to re-enter information, but once set up was nice tool for unwinding for sleep, calming squirrel brains that don't want to settle to sleep. Loved the gradual morning wake up for those of us who despise a harsh alarm. The app in general could use some better onboarding, tooltips, etc for a better user experience, but I'm a bit biased as a ux ui designer (but also, if. I don't find it intuitive and I design apps..... could use a little better flow for the newbie user to the app). Beautiful design to the actual Machine. Nice aesthetic, cost for membership if you choose advanced options is not cost prohibitive with nice long trial periods to see if its a fit. Recommend.

reviews.author: No name
reviews.title: I love it but the price is insane. App needs work
reviews.content_html:
Admittedly, I initially started a return for this product. The idea is so cool but the ridiculous price of this PLUS paying for the app? Absolutely insane. I originally started this return and bought the egg but I realized this one is the only one that allows for multiple alarms, which I need. It would not have been a reason to spend this $$$ but since I already had it, I kept it.

The app price makes a bit more sense, there are a lot of options, I do love the alarm options, the wind down and white noise. And it comes out to like $5 a month so whatever. But the price for the clock? There is no excuse for it.

Some nicer things...I freakin love it. I do. The app is not the best but I appreciate all it offers. But I won't let go that almost $170 for this is greedy. I'd be giving it 5 stars if it was the price it should be, maybe $70-80 at most.

reviews.title: WARNING: FORCED SUBSCRIPTION
reviews.content_html:
WARNING: FORCED SUBSCRIPTION
I have used hatch products for years and have always loved them. When one of my old ones bit the dust, I immediately ordered the new version because I have always loved it so much. Well apparently now you cannot use the product unless you auto subscribe to their monthly Hatch+!! Sure, you get a month free and you can cancel before you get charged, but wtf!? I shouldn’t have to enter my credit card info and subscribe to something I KNOW I don’t want, just to use a product I already paid for! This is SO shady, it’s such a scam to force people to do it, hoping they forget and you can charge them. Subscriptions are OUT OF CONTROL these days and this is so shady and gross.

reviews.author: Sandy Sailor
reviews.title: It's not great!!!
reviews.content_html:
The concept, aesthetic are good, execution is not good and notably cheap...I am not exagerating when I say total rip-off. For a $160 alarm clock; it does not have bluetooth in the year 2026... (Last I checked bluetooth in a speaker became standard in speakers 15 years ago circa ~2011.) I was a little confused why not...turns out, they charge you a ridiculous subscription for anything beyond 2 basic alarm noises, (they will also auto-enroll you into this rip-off subscription), the app UI is ridiculously confusing, and the routines are awfully hard to customize and come with ridiculous AI-slop content. If you do have the subscription, you just get more AI-slop, not much more functionality whatsoever... Do not fall for their fancy marketing, they totally brick the device unless you pay them a fee for a product that is way worse than an echo or nest speaker. You can't even so much as connect your spotify to it! They are not deserving of your business and should be fined by the treasury department for innapropriately advertising for people to use HSA funds as well. Under-insured American's should not be using their HSA funds, that they or their family may need for life-saving operations someday, on an alarm clock that is worse than alarm clocks that predate it by 15 years...

This was my first Amazon review (and I've bought probably 10's of thousands of $$ worth of products over the years on here), I'm that appalled.

reviews.author: Jacob
reviews.title: Beware of new app updates!
reviews.content_html:
They have updated their app and it is almost unusable now.

There is NO WAY for the alarm to turn the light and sound on at the same time.

I was hoping to use this as an “ok to wake” alarm for my child but currently the only option is for the light to turn on 5 minutes before the alarm goes off.

I don’t know anyone who would want their alarm clock light turning on 5 minutes before their alarm goes off.

I also confirmed this with their customer support team (of which they are not available via phone). Check recent reviews of the app in the App Store to see thousands of similar reviews.

I really wish I would have known this before purchasing a very expensive alarm clock!

reviews.author: TJFrench
reviews.title: It requires WiFi and app to function properly
reviews.content_html: When I first got this I would have given it 5 stars but unfortunately after having for almost two months I hate it. If I just based the review on the sound and lights it would be great but you have to have Wifi for it to function properly and for some reason this stupid thing will not stay connected when everything else in my house has no issues with my Internet. When it disconnects the clock stays on and lights the entire room and you can't turn it off. You can still turn on the sound and lights but only the sound you were already using. Really, when you lose the app it turns into any cheap sound machine. My recommendation, buy something cheaper that doesn't require an app to function properly.

reviews.author: MLWhite
reviews.title: Not for me.
reviews.content_html:
I was optimistic and willing to invest in changing my sleep and waking up habits- but this didn’t work for me at all. It can’t be adjusted to meet my needs enough. A few things frustrated me. I downloaded the app and was willing to pay for it, but there wasn’t much on it that appeals to me. Nothing especially unique, especially if you are someone who listens to audiobooks or podcasts already. If you already have some sort of white noise that you’re using that’s really what I found to be best. I found the light features frustrating because I’m light sensitive- they were too bright from first light. The dealbreaker for me was that I wanted to listen to one of their stories. The Great Gatsby. I know the book very well, and I was curious how their version went. I toggled to have the light be off because I wanted to fall asleep to just the story in a dark room. No matter how many different ways I tried to do the settings a bright green light came on. I get the metaphor. The green light. However, it was 11:30 at night and I wanted my room dark and I wanted to be able to listen to the story. I couldn’t the light go off and the story play (which I am paying for via the app) so instead, I listened to a podcast on my phone, and turned on a grey noise (free on the app). Why spend almost $200 on an alarm clock that you’re not using. It’s not adjustable enough. It’s not customizable enough. And the light is too bright. Maybe it’s too big. I woke up instantly to the light because the first light is bright. It’s not a gentle glow. The colors change, but it starts out bright, no matter the settings.
I guess live and learn. I am going to return it in about five minutes.

reviews.author: Cynthia Villalta
reviews.title: Make mornings better with this
reviews.content_html: I got it for myself, to stop using my phone as the alarm. The light and tweets make it so easy to get up. It starts your day off positive i my opinion. I use black out curtains to sleep and wake up before the sun rises, so this helps me get woken up in light as well as with nice sounds. It works so well, I got another one for my kids' room. They are very heavy sleepers and had positive effects the first day they used it. I like how you can adjust the duration of the sunrise, the volume and the sounds you are awoken to. Also it's great that if you snooze it, the light does not turn off, just the sound. Which is very helpful that you can enjoy a couple ,ore mins of sleep/ rest without falling back into a deep sleep. I also enjoy that it connects to Bluetooth so you can play your music from your phone on it. There is a clock on it, that can be dimmed or turned off all the way, which is very nice. They have music and sounds that you can use to go to sleep to, and an automatic turn off timer. There is also a variety of lights for a nightlight feature. All lights can be at the level of your likeness. You can also choose to set the alarm to only light, or only sound. It has a cord, but it also has battery ports, so I think that's awesome. I haven't used it just with batteries tho. I also did not put any in so it's nice that it's not necessary to work. All in all, I would definitely recommend, and I actually have recommended it to others. It doesn't take up too much space, the cord is a good lenth, and everything about this item and the features it brings to the table is just amazing. I'm so glad I didn't spend triple the price on the other name brand clock like this, because this was rated higher and is reasonably priced. Make your mornings better by waking up with this awesome Sunrise Alarm Clock

reviews.author: Bree
reviews.title: Pretty while it last
reviews.content_html:
I recently moved to an efficiency that has no windows so I wanted something to simulate a sunrise that way I wouldn’t be in the pitch dark in the morning or have to turn a light on. This works well, but because the sunrise only stays on for 60 minute … and goes out right after its short lived. Unless you get up immediately… I need something more customizable to stay on longer.

They do have the night light setting i believe last 240 minutes but you would have to physically turn it on after the sunrise goes out. I think I need the hatch after all.

I do think this is a great product, I tested the Bluetooth .. it’s not super loud but loud enough for me. The sunrise was beautiful to see while it lasted. It takes up a bit of room on nightstand. But it’s sturdy, and the pink is pretty. I should also note I thought it would help my dog to differentiate between morning and night … I looked .. he slept through it 😅.

🚨 in a pitch black room, when it’s rising and reaches its peak it reminds me more a sunset then a beautiful sunny day .. it’s like orangish and I set it to the brightest.

reviews.author: Cory Clayburn
reviews.title: Terrific product, but the speaker quality is being underutilized
reviews.content_html:
Excellent product. It works incredibly well, and the speaker quality is fantastic. However, the audio files built into the device are terrible quality. They put a very nice audio driver into the device, but cheaped out on memory.

As a Bluetooth speaker, it's incredibly punchy for it's size. But, as a peaceful alarm, the built in sounds are pretty harsh. To the designers, Id recommend either increasing the memory or adding an external storage input, like a micro SD. What's the point of a beefy driver if you're using hyper-compressed audio?

Aside from the audio quality, the fit and finish is great. It's very bright, the interface is intuitive, the buttons feel good. It feels high quality and solid. It's a terrific product, but the speaker is being held back by the poor preset recording quality!

There weren’t as many alarm sounds as sound machine sounds, and they all stressed me out. I do not want to wake up to the sound of birds chirping over spa music. I know some people find birds relaxing, but having ADD, it stresses me out. I like constant sounds, if that makes sense. The ones that get it, get it. Anyway, it has potential to be a great product if it’s your thing.

reviews.author: Lady Aerospace Engineer
reviews.title: It’s ok - not perfect..yet!
reviews.content_html:
I was looking for a sunrise alarm clock that was cheaper than Hatch and found this one. So far it’s ok - wish it worked better for the pricey price.

Beautiful shape and colors for sure. I tried setting it up for its alarm which ended up being confusing when I tested it. On sound only, it plays the chosen sound on time. On sound+light, it started the sunrise early based the duration amount you set up - meaning that amount earlier than the time you set up alarm time for. I found this a bit impractical and not really what I was looking for. I wanted the light and sound to start and build up at the same time at the time I set it up for.

The selection of sounds wasn’t as various as I expected and not really quality nature sounds either. Only 2 alarm sounds were actually somewhat relaxing (birds chirping and the waves on a shore) as nature sounds, rest were musical or the classic alarm - not relaxing for my taste. I just wanted multiple nature sounds really.

Last one: there is no weekday versus weekend mode. Your alarm is set the same every day of the week. A bit impractical IMHO.

I am hoping this clock lasts - it’s not heavy at all and so far seems fairly straightforward in setup with a bit of patience.

I plan on testing until I can no longer return it. Still on the fence - will update if I return it.

UPDATE: I am returning it and hope next revision incorporates these suggestions. So close to perfect!

reviews.author: Anton Lagman
reviews.title: Unreliable Alarm
reviews.content_html:
I really wanted to love this alarm clock because on paper it’s amazing. The sunrise lighting feature is honestly one of the best I’ve seen, it gradually brightens the room in a really natural, calming way and makes waking up feel way less harsh. The design is clean, the features are solid, and overall it feels like a premium product.

But there’s one major issue that completely ruins it: the alarm is unreliable unless I unplug the clock every single night. If I don’t reset it like that, there’s a good chance the alarm just won’t go off at all. That’s a dealbreaker for something whose main purpose is literally to wake me up.

It’s frustrating because everything else about this clock is great, but I can’t depend on it. An alarm clock that can’t reliably alarm defeats the whole point.

However, everal drawbacks make it frustrating to use. First, the power cord is very short. Unless you have an outlet exactly where you want it, this is an issue. I wrote to the seller asking where I can buy a longer cord that is compatible and received no help. Second, the birdsong nature sound- typically the one I would want to wake up to is intolerably irritating. There is literally a cuckoo included in the recording which chirps continuously. Finally, there is no battery backup option so when you lost power you must re-set both the alarm and the time completely.

reviews.author: G. Schmidt
reviews.title: Works but Complicated and Obsolete
reviews.content_html:
Overall, while this alarm does wake me it seems to be a bit obsolete. In a lot of ways it reminds me of a universal remote control from the late 90's in that it DOES work, but it's complicated to operate and missing more modern features. Additionally, the white light from the display numbers is a problem.

This thing has 11 buttons, two sliders, and two rollers. Many of the buttons do different things at different times or when the sliders are in different positions. Don't lose the manual. I've only had it a week, so I'm still learning it, but twice in the morning I've pulled the plug because I couldn't get the alarm to turn off in my sleepy confusion. To make matters worse, the buttons are all on the back so you have to pick the thing up and tilt it forward to read the buttons to know what they do - make sure you have your glasses handy and a light on.

It's also missing more modern conveniences that we've become accustomed to. Do you have different alarm times saved for weekends or for multiple times per day? Do you have varied Snooze times or different sounds/volumes for different alarms? None of that is possible with this alarm where you get one alarm time setting and an unvaried 9-minute Snooze. Changing it requires navigating those 15 controls again. The sounds are fine, but Alexa's ambient sounds are better and *way* more varied. With the exception of the wakeup sunrise effect, the rest of the lighting seems more of a gimmick than truly useful. The nightlights are too bright even when turned all the way down and half the colors are unsuited to being a night light at all.

The biggest oversight in this product, in my opinion, is that the clock time display doesn't self-adjust for room brightness. The white digits are themselves a problem for light sanitation. At max brightness you can read from them if you're close. You can turn down the brightness with one of those rollers when sleeping, but then you can't see it when when you're awake and the room lights are on. Short of manually adjusting brightness every time you sleep and wake, you're left with a display bright enough to cast shadows at night or an unreadable one during the day.

All that said, the sunrise effect does wake me, even from ten feet away. I was skeptical of that because I am generally very difficult to wake. The military taught me to sleep through most things but this gradual brightening and color progression does what is advertised - at least for me.

I know some of the other reviews praise the fact that this alarm does not require an app, but for me that's what is missing most (other than a light sensor to adjust that display brightness). I'm in my mid 50's, but I'm used to having a lot more - and a lot easier - control over my devices than this alarm provides. This device would have seemed cool 25 years ago, now it just feels obsolete. There's nothing it can do that Alexa and a smart bulb can't do better and easier.

I like everything else about the clock and the features it provided but if it doesn’t wake me up with that light I’m not going to use it.

reviews.title: An easy, peaceful wake up!
reviews.content_html:
I LOVE this sunrise alarm clock. I’ve used it for a week now and have woken up about halfway through the “sunrise” each morning, before the actual alarm sound goes off. I am not a morning person, but this has changed the game!

The size is great, it’s incredibly easy to use, and it looks good on my bedside table. It offers tons of different noises for sleeping and for waking up. I personally love the bird noises! I also love that you can adjust the brightness of the time display.

It’s definitely not the sturdiest thing in the world, but it seems like it will hold up!

Once they finally opened them and set them up we couldn't wait to see how they did in the morning. So here's the thing, one of them is great, easy to set the time, change music, hit snooze etc. It worked great! The other one about a week in stopped working. You can push the buttons to try to change time, music, hit snooze but it doesn't do anything. You have to actually unplug it to get it to turn off. I know i'm well outside of the return window and that's on me, however it's extremely frustrating. I gave it three stars, because one worked, and the other doesn't. It's literally a 50/50 chance. I'm now needing to purchase another alarm clock to replace this one, but i'm not sure I want to purchase this one again.

reviews.author: Adriana
reviews.title: Highly recommended!
reviews.content_html:
I’ve had this clock for about 6 weeks and, so far, I’m VERY satisfied both with the product and with the brand’s customer service.

I read the instructions in order to learn all the functions and it took me a bit of practice to learn the buttons without looking (it has many buttons and functions, and sometimes I need to access the functions in the dark, when I’m in bed and I don’t want to turn on the lights).

Customer service has been outstanding. I wrote an email to get my warranty card. They answered right away and they needed more information than what I gave them initially about the purchase. I honestly forgot to check for their reply for a few days, but they had answered immediately and had actually been following up with me, writing several emails, just to make sure that they got my information so that I’d get the warranty card. I got it right after I sent them the missing information.

Besides using it as a sunrise alarm, I like that I can use it as a very dim or very bright light depending on my needs, as well as a reading light while in bed and a radio in the morning. Also, there’s a warm light mode that flickers like a candle and I find it relaxing when winding down from the day. There are many other light modes, both static and dynamic, which I use according to my mood, time of day, and needs (for instance, after waking with the sunrise alarm, I sometimes turn on a cool-toned light mode to help me through the haze right after waking up, and I use it as a lamp when I need more light than the bedside lamps provide). I also love that I can turn off the red time display, because I don’t like lights from electronics on my field of vision while I’m sleeping.

I bought the wood version, which I figured would feel cozier in a bedroom than an all white one. I was hoping that it would be bamboo, but it’s plastic, similar to the old clocks from the 70s through the early 90s. It gives it a somewhat quirky vintage feel that contrasts with the more modern and white round all-lit face. If you’re looking for something that looks sleek, I’d go with the all white version.

I gave this product 5 stars because, on top of the great customer service, it has an excellent quality, with a lot of functions and options, at a very low price when compared to similar products from other brands. The only thing I would improve is the sound quality and adding AM radio (there are still some interesting AM stations around!), but that’s not enough to knock down any stars. An enhancement would be to add backlit buttons which can be turned on and off (like the time display), so that the controls are easier to navigate when in the dark. However, I imagine that those things would make the product more expensive.

All in all, highly recommended!

There are many other light modes, both static and dynamic, which I use according to my mood, time of day, and needs (for instance, after waking with the sunrise alarm, I sometimes turn on a cool-toned light mode to help me through the haze right after waking up, and I use it as a lamp when I need more light than the bedside lamps provide). I also love that I can turn off the red time display, because I don’t like lights from electronics on my field of vision while I’m sleeping.

I bought the wood version, which I figured would feel cozier in a bedroom than an all white one. I was hoping that it would be bamboo, but it’s plastic, similar to the old clocks from the 70s through the early 90s. It gives it a somewhat quirky vintage feel that contrasts with the more modern and white round all-lit face. If you’re looking for something that looks sleek, I’d go with the all white version.

I gave this product 5 stars because, on top of the great customer service, it has an excellent quality, with a lot of functions and options, at a very low price when compared to similar products from other brands. The only thing I would improve is the sound quality and adding AM radio (there are still some interesting AM stations around!), but that’s not enough to knock down any stars. An enhancement would be to add backlit buttons which can be turned on and off (like the time display), so that the controls are easier to navigate when in the dark. However, I imagine that those things would make the product more expensive.

All in all, highly recommended!

reviews.author: Bonnie
reviews.title: I finally found an alarm that successfully wakes me up!
reviews.content_html:
I LOVE this sunrise alarm clock! I was comparing this one to others that are more expensive, bought and then originally returned this one thinking I’d like the Swarmir Smart Light Noise Machine that has a phone app and more color features. I immediately regretted returning this one after trying out the sleep-aid and wake-up alarm functions on the Swarmir. While the other color options for ambience and the phone app are awesome, there are several things I like WAY more about the Jall and bought this one again!

PROS / What the Jall Wake-Up Light alarm does better:
- I find the manual buttons are easier to use on this one vs. the Swarmir (though the phone app for Swarmir is also easy to use).
- I LOVE the wake-up alarm sound options much more. The wind chimes sound is my favorite and none of the others that I looked at had it.
- The sleep aid for the Jall clock ACTUALLY helps me fall asleep with the sound and light gradually decreasing until they softly go off. The Swarmir does decrease sound volume and light brightness too but suddenly cuts both off when the timer ends, which is jarring and wakes me right back up again.
- I surprisingly prefer not having an app to control it, I need this alarm to force me to get up and turn it off. The whole reason I wanted a sunrise alarm was because 1/2 asleep me is too cunning for the iPhone or other phone alarm apps. Phone alarm goes off, 1/2 asleep me taps the phone without getting out of bed, then falls back to sleep within seconds. The ability to turn off the Swarmir alarm with its app causes the same problem—a quick tap on the app while I'm still in bed and it’s off while I pass out again. I set up this Jall clock across the room so 1/2 asleep me has no choice but to get out of bed.
- Jall has a battery that saves your pre-set alarms, sound choices, and volume settings. The clock cannot run on the battery alone (has to be plugged into an outlet to work) but if you unplug it to move it somewhere else it will turn back on with the correct time and your settings still saved.

CONS / What the Swarmir does better and what the Amazon description for Jall does not make clear:
- With this one, you cannot use any of the other color settings with the alarm clock, they are purely ambience lighting you can turn-on whenever, but they are separate from the alarm clock. The alarm only uses the orange/yellow/golden light with the alarm to simulate the sunrise (which is still nice).
- You can set the Jall alarm sound to FM radio BUT you cannot choose any channel you want. The clock will automatically scan for stations that come in clearly enough and will pre-set them itself. You can then choose from those 20 pre-set stations, but if your favorite stations do not come in clearly enough the Jall clock will skip over them. I liked maybe 2 out of the 20 that it pre-set for me.
- If you're looking for the super colorful, swirling, rainbow fading in and out ambience lighting, this Jall clock is not for you. You can set solid colors or set it to shift through all the colors but they stay solid (fading to one color at a time). This is where the Swarmir one has the Jall beat big time—the color features with the Swarmir are gorgeous.
- The bottom 1/2 of the Jall clock where the time and LED display show do not light up or show the colors (that part stays dark and shadowy). The Swarmir has a full light-up display so the entire circle is colorful.

TL/DR: While this Jall Wake-Up Light does not have a phone app and does not have all the other color modes for ambience lighting like the Swarmir, I very much prefer the wake-up and sleep sounds, and it’s actually doing its job by waking me up on time in a way that’s pleasant (and doesn’t make me want to throw the alarm out of my 3rd story apartment window 💜). Full disclosure: I did also buy the Swarmir Smart White Noise Machine for the colorful settings and ambience, and I use both every day—Jall to wake-up and fall asleep, and Swarmir for ambience lighting, white noise while I work, and for daily meditation.

**Will update with a video that plays each wake up and fall asleep sound option from this Jall model vs. the other model I also bought for those of you interested.

reviews.author: MayhemHatter
reviews.title: Wonderful!
reviews.content_html:
I have wanted a sunrise/sunset simulation alarm for years but could never justify it while I was a college student. Now that I have been out of school for a few years and settled into my career I finally took the plunge and I am so happy that I did. This is one of the most valuable purchases I have made in my adult life.

Getting up in the morning has never really been difficult for me. I am an early bird for sure. I haven't slept in past 9 in probably 5 years. In the summer I usually bound out of bed as the sun rises. In the winter? Not so much. It is dark and cold and even the most gentle alarm from my phone would jarr me awake. I have had this alarm for about 2 weeks now and it is the dead of winter and I have woken up feeling refreshed every morning, which translates to more energy and focus throughout my day.

I really like the design. It is simple and minimal - something I am slowly transitioning more of my life towards. It was also really easy to set up. Those reviews saying it was too hard and giving it a bad review obviously didn't read the instructions. I skimmed through the manual and had it figured out in about 10 minutes.

The front display has adjustable brightness which is a huge plus. There are 3 settings: bright, middle, and off. I keep mine in the middle. I appreciate that the display is an orange color - not blue or white. Less pain to the eyes if you have to check it in the middle of the night.

You can set 2 different sunrise alarms which is helpful since I wake up later 2 days off my week and like that I don't have to reprogram it for those mornings, I just hit the button for which alarm I need. The sunrise alarm can be adjusted in a variety of ways:
- time (duh)
- alarm sound (I like the bird song but there are a handful of other choices)
- brightness level: it always starts from off then you can adjust how bright it gets by the time the alarm actually starts at you programmed time.
- how far before your alarm it begins to brighten

I have my "sunrise" programmed to start 15 minutes before my alarm is set to start. It goes from off to a deep red/orange to yellow and finally to white. The maximum brightness will absolutely light up a whole room. That is a bit much for me so I have it turned back just a little. I am typically awake sometime around yellow. This wakes me up so much more gently and I have a few minutes in bed before the "alarm" of birdsong actually gets me up. So I am awake before my alarm most days and getting out of bed right after my alarm goes off. No more poking at my phone for 10 minutes trying to get myself out of bed in the morning.

The "sunset" can also be programmed, however there is only one setting, not 2 as with the morning alarms. It can be adjusted to your liking in the following ways:
- how long the "sunset" lasts
- starting brightness
- sounds played (less options here than the sunrise but more relaxing, I like the rain the most)

From the starting brightness that you program over the designated amount of time it slowly dims from white to yellow to orange to red and finally off. I have mine set for 20 minutes and I typically read a paper book as the blue light from screens is counter-productive. Once I stop being able to easily read that is usually about when my body is drifting towards sleep. It finished darkening while I snuggle fully into bed and off I go to dreamland.

It does have the ability to change the color of the light but the default works just fine for me.

I absolutely love this product. I do have 2 small qualms but nothing to take away from a 5 star review. I notice it more in during the "sunset" but the transition from light to dark is not very smooth, I definitely notice it click down with each level. It may be due to my settings and I will see if having a longer period of time to transition from light to dark with smooth that out. My other issue is in the mornings. Once my alarm goes off and it is at full brightness I wish there was a way to turn off the alarm sound but keep the light on. There is a "snooze" button but I haven't tried it yet so I may experiment with that.

In the short time I have had this product I can honestly say it has changed my morning routine and also the effects on the rest of my day. My sleep is more restful and waking up is so easy. It has really helped my mental health and I literally cannot stop raving about it to friends and family. If you are considering purchasing a product like this I highly recommend starting here. There are more much more expensive models but this one is accessible and user friendly.

reviews.author: James Mitchell
reviews.title: Better than my phone, but a couple minutes fast after a year.
reviews.content_html: Much easier to wake up to than the alarm on your phone or any other normal alarm clock. Worth the money in my opinion. I really like the ability to set two separate alarm times that can be applied to weekdays, weekends, or the whole week. Only complaint is that after about a year of having it, I've notice that it is fast. I have to set it back a couple minutes every 6 months or so. Really not that big of a deal, but could be improved. Definitely better than being slow.

reviews.author: Maria Korolov
reviews.title: Works great but NOT easy to program.
reviews.content_html:
The clock itself is great. Works perfectly. Love the light.

The user manual is incomprehensible and doesn't 100% match the actual functionality of the clock! At least, the manual linked to here doesn't fully match -- maybe I have a slightly different version of the clock? I no longer have the original paper manual it came with because who keeps those things.

The clock is NOT easy to use. There are a bunch of buttons around the top, and the labels aren't intuitive. In this day and age, there's no reason for that!

Whenever I have to change anything -- or I unplug it and move it somewhere else -- I have to find the manual, and go through a long trial-and-error process to try to get it to work.

I'm guessing they're selling a lot of units of this clock. Judging by the number of reviews, there are a lot of folks using it. Maybe create an app to go with it? Something similar to the apps that Roombas have? These days, writing an app is a trivial task, and the alarm already has Bluetooth built in.

Or use the built-in LED display to create a better, more logical menu system.

Myself, I want to do three things:

1. Set the time
2. Set the alarm
3. Turn the light on and off in case I just want to use it as a lamp.

It took me half an hour of fiddling this morning to figure out just how to turn it on to work as a lamp. Once I did it, I immediately forgot how. I'm going to have to practice this for a few days to cement it into my brain.

reviews.author: KC
reviews.title: It works well but.....
reviews.content_html:
I always wanted to try one of these but I'm cheap and in the summer I can just leave a shade open and get woken up by light. I should have bought one of these for the winter but I'm using it now and it does work. The light getting brighter seems to wake me up more gently than an alarm and for whatever reason that feels better. So I like what this does but I do have some issues with it.

First nitpick it's pretty large and it takes up room on the night stand. On the plus side it can work as a light so I probably could remove the light that's there. I also wonder if I could just put it farther away on the dresser. The instructions say to have it closer but you can have it get pretty bright so I think it might work and I'll have to try that.

Second is that it's kind of annoying to set up. Maybe because I haven't dealt with an alarm clock in years but this thing could really use an app to set it up and I'm sure they have a pricier one with that and I should have checked and sprung for it. Now that I have it set up it's over but changing anything will be a pain.

I could maybe nitpick more but overall this seems to be a good product that does what you expect.

reviews.author: Charles Sorgie
reviews.title: Pretty cool so far
reviews.content_html:
Still playing with it…

As others have said, the buttons on the edge take some getting used to. It helps to remember 3, 10/2 and 11/1 for the clock positions of the buttons, but not by much. There’s a sunset button, two alarm buttons, a group of three light toggle/bright/dim buttons, and a group of three FM toggle/vol+/vol- buttons. That’s a lot of buttons. There’s snooze “area” at 12 that you just need to pass your hand over.

I won’t repeat everything in the manual but this while stated isn’t obvious:

* I’ll assume you’ve configured the alarms to use both the light and the sound effects.
* In order to set an alarm, you have to use a side button.
* When the alarm goes off
** you have to turn off the light using a side button.
** you can use the menu button to silence the alarm and keep it set.
** you can use the side button to silence the alarm and turn it off.

So, intelligently, the alarm goes off, you touch menu and get out of bed with the light on, then you turn the light off, and you’re set for tomorrow. If you don’t want the alarm to go off tomorrow, click the side button. This is easier than fumbling for a side button while in bed.

It’s pretty nifty and the colors and transitions are super smooth. The base is heavy too and stays put.

reviews.author: Cassie G.
reviews.title: Unusable product due to missing basic information
reviews.content_html:
This listing fails to mention one of the most basic and critical details: the power plug type.

There is no mention of it in the description, specifications, or anywhere on the page, and it is not shown in any of the photos.

For a product sold internationally, that is completely unacceptable.

Like most people, I reasonably assumed this would be USB powered and compatible for international use, as is standard for modern alarm clocks and small electronics. Instead, it arrived with a US two-pin plug that cannot be used in Australia without an adapter, making it unusable straight out of the box.

I spent $293.29 AUD on this and cannot even plug it in.

This is not a minor oversight, it is a fundamental product detail that should be clearly stated. Very disappointing experience.

reviews.author: ejhs
reviews.title: Beware if you’re replacing an older version
reviews.content_html:
I’ve had a Philips HF3520 since 2018 and i LOVED it, but it died on me.

I bought a replacement and was so disappointed to find that it was very different from the original. It has a different finish and it feels really flimsy and cheap in comparison. I used it once- the light was cooler, had a terrible flicker and even the bird sounds were different and awful.

I’m not sure if they sent me a counterfeit or if Philips has gone way down in quality. Either way, this version was a total disappointment and definitely not worth the money. Wish I could buy one of the old ones again.

Edit- if you have an old version that appears to die, try heating it up for a few minutes. Apparently there’s a chip that can stop working when it gets cold. I set a heating pad under my old version for about 10 minutes and it started working again, good as new, no problems since.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.title: Frustrating design flaws
reviews.content_html:
The light function and radio work great and it's easy generally easy to use. It's quite easy to set the time and alarm, the touch controls are sensitive and has very straight forward controls which is nice... but with a large caveat-

The issue is the general shape and button size / placement. Because it's slightly tilted, every time you want to adjust anything or press a button, this thing slides around like a banana peel on my side table. The buttons on top are far too small for pressing when half asleep. It's too easy to accidentally turn the alarm off instead of snoozing. It needs to be heavier or shaped differently with the buttons in a different location. The battery is fine, I just keep it plugged in.

Also, for some with serious "alarm anxiety," I cannot STAND that the alarm time is not constantly displayed only the alarm icon and current time is displayed.

reviews.author: Vanellope
reviews.title: Terrible PWM (flickering)
reviews.content_html: This clock has absolutely terrible PWM (flickering) at any brightness level - even at full 100% brightness (level 20). At first, I couldn’t figure out what was causing me awful migraines right in the morning, but then I narrowed it down to this clock. The attached video is a slow motion capture at 120 Hz (mains flicker is 60 Hz and human eye can perceive 30 Hz). While this is four times faster than the human eye can see, the flicker does affect sensitive individuals, such as myself. I would stay far away from this clock if you are sensitive to flickering lights. For something that costs $150, this is completely unacceptable. The attached video shows the flicker.

reviews.author: Katherine Orr
reviews.title: More than an alarm clock, an invitation to disconnect and rest
reviews.content_html:
The difference is immediate! I just got my Hatch Restore 3 last week and am already obsessed (and telling all my friends!). I live on a noisy street so I was considering just getting a sound machine, but having the alarm clock, all the different sound options, the light options is incredible. I actually sleep with my phone across the room now, which, even if psychological, allows me to breathe deeper and truly feel disconnected from all the mental clutter that comes from having my phone so close when I sleep. I love the alarm sounds (the sunrise is my fav!), the rain sounds to fall asleep and the whole wind-down routine.

I'm excited to keep exploring all that my new Hatch can offer and keep getting better sleep!

reviews.author: Marlo
reviews.title: Perfect for the Winter season!!! However…
reviews.content_html: I bought this clock because it has been extra difficult for me to wake up in the morning because it is wayyyy too dark outside. This clock wakes me up slowly and wakes me up in such a good mood! Setting up the clock was a little challenging just because there’s so much going on and I wasn’t sure where to go to set up my alarms, choose music, etc. After a few attempts I finally got the routines down and loved it! The only thing is that there are many other features such as alarm sounds, winding down music, etc. that is only available if you pay for a subscription. For how much you’re paying for this clock, you would wish that the subscription would come with the clock. However, I wouldn’t say you need the subscription if you just want to use the clock for its main purpose. The add ons aren’t necessary but probably would be nice to have. Overall, I definitely recommend it, IF YOUR BUDGET ALLOWS FOR IT! It has done wonders for me, but there could be other options that can work for others with different budget concerns.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.title: Better after tweaking
reviews.content_html:
Edit 12/6/2025: I did some brainstorming for how to get around the problems I had, and tried it out for a few days. In the process I ended up finding some solutions that I felt worked very well and were worth sharing.

If I set up the cue and just leave it running, it will keep going until I start the unwind routine. That works well enough for a bedtime reminder that doesn't go away. My biggest issue was not being to set the time for things in the "undwind" part, since I use that to be aware of how long I've been awake (IF I've been awake too long). I've gotten around this by setting up several of the fixed time unwind steps together, so they'll play one after another and the combined time is close to what I'm looking for. Based on my experiments, this actually works quite well. One of the limitations of the process I was using before (setting audible to run for a set amount of time on my phone) was that you can't really set up anything to play in a list--you'd have to fiddle with your phone again to fix it if you needed anything extra before/after the story. With this, I can set a 5 or 10 minute meditation, and then a 30 ish minute sleep story for after I've relaxed more.
I still think there should be more flexibility in how you can set things up--setting the amount of time the alarm goes off, or the snooze time, or being able to use sounds from different categories for other steps would be useful. It is also expensive and susbscription based for the full experience. And I also highly recommend setting it up well in advance of bedtime with a lot of time to play around and test sounds on the device, because you don't want to set something up and have it wake you up on accident because of the volume you left it at.
But the sounds are very high quality, and I've found I like the set up after I've tweaked it to suit my needs better. With that in mind, I'm going to bump my review up a star.
A suggestion for the developers--you might be able to reduce friction like this if you included some examples (maybe a video?) of how people might use the alarm in somewhat different ways. I like to do everything else I need before bed while the cue is playing, then come back, press the button, and listen to the meditation while I'm in bed. Then it proceeds to a short story, and then some extra music that goes for a set amount of time, so I know how long I've been awake if I hear it end. I'm guessing that maybe some people listen to some of their sounds/meditations outside of bed, and they might have only one sound per step or they could have a bunch. Some people may have it right next to their bedside, while others (like me) might have it away from their bed so that it encourages them to get up in the morning. It didn't really occur to me when I first got this that I could use it in the way that I am now. Seeing some examples of different ways people used it might've prevented some of my initial frustration.

Original:
I wanted to like this. I got this because I thought it would assist with my attempt to no longer use my phone as an alarm and just use a sunrise alarm. But for an alarm that's so expensive AND requires a subscription to get full use out of, it's remarkably feature poor. It has different steps for bedtime and wake up--cue to unwind, unwind, and sleep, and wake up. I liked the idea of this, but how they have it structured is very restrictive and isn't what I was looking for.

You can't choose whichever audio you want for each step in their process. They have specific audio for each of their steps, and you can't mix them which is frustrating because if you could you might actually be able to make some good flexible bedtime routines. You're stuck with whatever they think belongs in each category. Also I think their library all of the time, which might be good in some ways, but won't that mean that the things I like will be going away as well? Good bedtimes are all about consistency so why such a focus on changing everything?

You can't control how long anything other than the sleep step plays for. If you want your "unwind" step to only play for 5 minutes, or to play for 45 minutes, you'd need to get up and manually set it or fiddle with your phone while it's playing and you were trying to sleep. Unless there's a feature that's hidden somewhere? I don't think so, though. The steps generally say "this is a fixed duration". I use sleep stories to both help relax and let me know how long it's taken me to fall asleep, so being unable to control it at all is a problem.

You can't schedule anything other than the alarm and your "cue", which I don't think you even get unless you're a plus subscriber. If you wanted this to double as a lamp with a scheduled on and off time to assist with going to bed, you can't. You can't even schedule your unwind to start at a certain time. You must walk up to it and press a button or start it from the app (not good for trying to get off of phone).

I don't think you can even adjust how long your alarm will ring, snooze time, whether the snooze stops the light or the sound or both.

The app also often has long response times and takes a long time to save changes.

Overall:
It's a sunrise alarm. The light is good, and some of the sounds offered are interesting. It's very expensive and requires a subscription for full functionality, when there are other sunrise alarms out there for half of the price (or even less) that are more flexible. I feel very disappointed in it and I'm thinking about returning it. If you are from Hatch and see that I'm wrong about the features, please let me know. I wanted to like it. Not being able to set the duration of steps, not being able to schedule times of different steps, not being able to select what sounds I'd like for each step (why can't I start some of the sleep sounds inside the unwind step? having them start out of the blue after unwind will end up waking me up), and not being able to adjust basic things like snooze and alarm duration time are kind of deal breakers for me. This is functionality that exists in much cheaper models.

I get the feeling that a lot of the positive reviews are from people who've never used a smart sunrise alarm before.

reviews.author: Paige
reviews.title: Buy it!!
reviews.content_html:
I can honestly say this sunrise alarm clock is the best purchase I’ve ever made. I love falling asleep to rain sounds, but using the TV meant I could never get my room completely dark—which always affected my sleep. With this clock, I finally get the best of both worlds: relaxing sounds and a pitch-black room.

Ever since I started using it, I’ve been sleeping so much better. The variety of sounds is amazing, and I love being able to choose different light colors to match my mood. Waking up to a gentle sunrise instead of a harsh alarm has completely changed my mornings too.

If you’re someone who struggles with sleep or just wants a more peaceful nighttime routine, I can’t recommend this enough.
