reviews.author: Dee
reviews.title: Fails at alarm clocking, wins at aesthetics.
reviews.content_html:
This is a beautiful bedside clock sound machine if you enjoy wonderful lights. It, however, is not the best alarm clock. It lacks basic features that an alarm clock should have: namely, the ability to stop the damn alarm without turning off the alarm settings completely for the next day.

The only way to “stop the alarm,” is to hit the snooze button, which isn’t exactly effective. By name alone, snooze means you are quieting the alarm for a specified amount of time but once that time has been breached, the alarm goes off again. The only way to completely end the alarm is to turn the alarm off all together, which seems a recipe for disaster because to ensure it is on for the next day, one would have to manually turn it back on. This seems like a recipe for disaster in so many ways.

1.) If you are someone that gets up and gets moving immediately, for fifteen minutes your alarm will continue when you’ve long left the room. Not so bad for single sleepers, but with anyone that has a partner with an opposing schedule, this is a possible disruption for them.

2.) If you turn it off completely; there’s a chance you won’t remember to reset it for the following day which may cause any number of chaotic issues.

This main function is why this is a terrible alarm clock. It doesn’t achieve that very basic function. While the sleep music is great, I already have a Sonos speaker, a Google Nest, and my iPhone along with the fiftyleven number of apps that can handle my sleep music. I’d rather they have perfected the core functionality of an alarm (and sunlamp) before they bloated this thing with other features.

Fortunately, it is a nice looking clock and the colors are pretty enough to endure the utterly flawed design. For the price, it is a decent sub. However, in the meantime, I may continue to looking for something that handles alarms much better.

reviews.author: Paola Luna
reviews.title: Aesthetic and Easy to Use, But Not Effective for Noise Cancellation
reviews.content_html:
I purchased this sunrise alarm clock with high hopes, especially after waiting several days for a price drop—unfortunately, only the putty color went on sale, and I really preferred the sage green. Still, I decided to order it, expecting it to meet my needs.

My main reason for buying this product was to drown out the noise from my upstairs neighbors in my small studio apartment. They have children and are noisy at all hours, so I wanted a device with speaker functionality that could play white noise, rain sounds, or music from my Spotify account to help me sleep better.

Unfortunately, I found the speaker performance to be very underwhelming. Despite setting it to maximum volume on the device itself and through my phone, it didn’t make much difference. I still had to keep my fan on to help drown out the noise because the speaker’s volume and quality are not enough to block out the sounds from upstairs. It’s almost the same volume as my iPhone, which I was previously using. This product did not meet my expectations or needs in that regard, and I’m quite disappointed.

Aesthetically, the device looks great and matches my decor perfectly. The packaging was excellent—organized, secure during transport, and easy to unbox. The quick start guide was straightforward and easy to understand, especially for someone comfortable with technology. I do enjoy the ambience it creates, and I appreciate the sunrise and sunset features, though I haven't tested them fully since my blinds are closed, and it's already bright enough during the day in my room.

I had heard great things about this product and even gifted a similar one to a family member during Christmas, which I liked very much. That’s why I was excited to try this model myself, but overall, I’m underwhelmed. If you’re looking for a device specifically to drown out noise, this probably isn’t the right choice. However, if you want an aesthetically pleasing light and sound machine for ambient purposes or to use as a wake-up clock, it does work well, just not for noise cancellation.

reviews.author: Kirbyjakobs
reviews.title: Highly Recommend. Wake up refreshed not grumpy and startled.
reviews.content_html:
I really like this alarm clock-especially for the money! I had originally looked at the original daylight alarm clock type brands but just couldn’t justify spending the price. So a few months ago I took the plunge to try this one. I actually purchased several to contrast and compare but once I tried this one I liked it so much I didn’t bother with the others and sent them back.

Full Disclosure: I HATE being woken up by a startling sound and I also DESPISE daylight savings time, but I digress.😜 In my effort to try to get back to a semblance of the wake up with the sun routine, my test started.

The first thing I noticed is that this thing is easy to set without instructions. It was surprisingly intuitive and I am pretty opinionated about my frustrations with such things on a regular basis!

How my particular alarm works with a half hour sunlight routine: If my alarm is set for 7:30am for example, at 7am the clock will start the process of gradually putting out more light… a very mild glow that grows slowly over the next half hour. At the 7:15 stage it seems to speed up the process of gradually glowing more imbut again nothing startling, just an ever so slightly brighter warm glow. It never makes you jump or startle. I’ve never once gotten that feeling of someone coming in and turning on all the lights.

At the actual alarm time, if you have chosen music to add, it starts. Eight options including birds chirping and roosters crowing are available options from which to choose. Surprisingly I chose birds chirping and like it very much although on those first few mornings when I was already at the other end of the house when the birds started chirping it was a tad puzzling.

Mine is set on a 30 minute increase and you are able to adjust the light coming on from as little as 5 minutes to a full 60 minutes. I just chose 30 minutes as it made sense to me as a place to start.

I was concerned this wouldn’t be harsh enough to wake me so set back up alarms across the room at first but have never had to use them.

I wake up happier-not startled or grumpy-and it truly does feel more natural even though it remains pitch dark outside. Last but not least not spending the price of the original version of this names me VERY happy. Highlight recommend.
reviews.content_text:
I really like this alarm clock-especially for the money! I had originally looked at the original daylight alarm clock type brands but just couldn’t justify spending the price. So a few months ago I took the plunge to try this one. I actually purchased several to contrast and compare but once I tried this one I liked it so much I didn’t bother with the others and sent them back.

Full Disclosure: I HATE being woken up by a startling sound and I also DESPISE daylight savings time, but I digress.😜  In my effort to try to get back to a semblance of the wake up with the sun routine, my test started.

The first thing I noticed is that this thing is easy to set without instructions. It was surprisingly intuitive and I am pretty opinionated about my frustrations with such things on a regular basis!

How my particular alarm works with a half hour sunlight routine: If my alarm is set for 7:30am for example, at 7am the clock will start the process of gradually putting out more light… a very mild glow that grows slowly over the next half hour. At the 7:15 stage it seems to speed up the process of gradually glowing more imbut again nothing startling, just an ever so slightly brighter warm glow. It never makes you jump or startle. I’ve never once gotten that feeling of someone coming in and turning on all the lights.

At the actual alarm time, if you have chosen music to add, it starts. Eight options including birds chirping and roosters crowing are available options from which to choose. Surprisingly I chose birds chirping and like it very much although on those first few mornings when I was already at the other end of the house when the birds started chirping it was a tad puzzling.

Mine is set on a 30 minute increase and you are able to adjust the light coming on from as little as 5 minutes to a full 60 minutes. I just chose 30 minutes as it made sense to me as a place to start.

I was concerned this wouldn’t be harsh enough to wake me so set back up alarms across the room at first but have never had to use them.

I wake up happier-not startled or grumpy-and it truly does feel more natural even though it remains pitch dark outside.  Last but not least not spending the price of the original version of this names me VERY happy.  Highlight recommend.

reviews.author: DB
reviews.title: Nice looking, well functioning clock.
reviews.content_html: It's a nice-looking clock for the price. As others have mentioned, it has several lights and sounds. The wake-up light is a great feature, and the sound quality is decent. The only thing I wish it had was a slower volume increase on the alarm sound. The theory is that it doesn't jolt you awake. I found that it starts at a low volume, but rises to its regular volume in about five seconds. I would have preferred a much more gradual volume increase - in the 20 to 30 second range. That said, it's a good alternative to your phone or "regular" alarm clock.

reviews.author: K. VanZalen
reviews.title: 10 yrs still works like new
reviews.content_html:
Purchased: 2016
Age: 10 yrs going strong

This has been the best addition to my lifestyle, completely changing how I wake up. Since changing to using a wake up light, I wake up more easily, and feeling more well rested (as you’re woken gradually not suddenly). It especially helps during the winter months.

If you’re looking for a reliable, long-lasting alarm with a gradual wake-up and calm-down light, this is well worth the price. It’s very bright and has a simple selection of pleasant nature based alarm choices.

The make and quality are exceptional, with a solid build that feels dependable. It carries strong brand recognition, which gave me confidence in my purchase, and it has certainly lived up to the reputation. Over ten years, the front buttons have only darkened slightly, and it still looks and feels almost new. In the last year, I’ve noticed the side buttons occasionally need an extra or firmer push, but everything else works perfectly without issue.

If you are on the fence - just do it, you will not regret it!

reviews.author: Belle
reviews.title: Be nice to yourself - kick the alarm and get this light!
reviews.content_html:
I was on the fence about this but after being YANKED awake by my awful phone alarm for the millionth time, I said 'just order it and try it!' and oh. my. goodness.
I am SO happy with this light. It DOES get bright enough to wake you, I promise; it starts to wake me when it's at half brightness so I get to have a sleepy mumble grumble and roll over, knowing that I still have 15-20 minutes left to wake up properly.
And then at the alarm time, it is at full brightness with birds chirping and a little music (we chose that from the 6 or 7 options) and it just makes every day easier.

Some people say they struggle with the buttons and wish it had an app like others but I promise you this is SO easy to set and change and turn off - it's worth every penny and more.

reviews.author: Aberante
reviews.title: The lighting wakes you up softly
reviews.content_html: I believe there are no better alarms out there. It's reliable. You set it up once, and you forget it. It lasts forever! I have one that is 10 years old and still going. The sounds are calming yet powerful and the lighting wakes you up softly unless you wait for 30mins, at which point the light is incredibly bright. I have 5 so far, one for each member of my family.

reviews.author: Wordna
reviews.title: Ahhh, a peaceful way to wake up.
reviews.content_html:
Although I had to knock off one star for the clunky controls and settings for this wake up light, I have to say that I really love using it. I got tired of waking up to annoying cell phone alarms for years, and wanted to check out this method. It really makes a huge difference using this, because you are not jolted awake with a sense of panic or urgency. You just have the light, which slowly turns on and begins brightening over the course of 30 minutes before the time you set it to wake you. Usually, this is enough to wake me up on its own and I turn it off before the auditory part of the alarm even goes off. But if you wait until it does go off, you wake to the peaceful sound of birds chirping and it feels like you are in nature. You have other options for the audio but that one is my favorite.

This was $89.95 when I bought it (it's gone up since then to $108.95 at the time of writing this review), which I still consider expensive for an alarm clock with a light. But I absolutely love it and would buy it again.

reviews.author: katherine S
reviews.title: Great alarm clock
reviews.content_html: This alarm clock is great for people who wake up before the sun rises. The light is bright and the sound slowly increases. The colors are beautiful and it doesn’t get hot. Easy to use and lightweight. Only getting four stars because you have to turn the alarm on everyday rather than setting a repeating schedule for when to wake you up.

reviews.author: Max Marin
reviews.title: Demonstration mode only plays radio, but the alarm does play chosen alarm sound.
reviews.content_html:
The clock has a really pleasant warm glow to it that really does wake me up before it plays the sound (which I like) and it doesn't seem to disturb my fiancée at all. I also really enjoy the go to sleep mode it has where it just does the sunrise in reverse. Overall, I do enjoy this clock.

It does have a couple drawbacks though. First, you cannot link a different alarm sound to the 2 alarm profiles available. And second, and this is probably the most frustrating one (at least on initial set up), the clock does NOT play your alarm sound in demonstration mode. It will only play the radio in demonstration mode. This is not what the instructions say. In the instructions it tells you that in demonstration mode, it will perform the simulated sunrise and play your chosen alarm sound over the course of 90 seconds. When actually using your chosen alarm profile, it WILL play your chosen alarm sound, so it does function as designed.

All in all, I would expect a $100 device to at least have correct instructions and maybe the additional functionality of having 2 completely unique alarm profiles rather than just being able to change the time and light intensity between the two profiles and not the sound. That said, I do still very much enjoy the clock and will be keeping it on my dresser.

reviews.author: Skylar Linkes
reviews.title: Love this but arrived damaged
reviews.content_html: I love this product! I have a lot of trouble waking up in the morning! I’m in the process of being tested for narcolepsy/idiopathic hypersomnia and it still manages to stir me awake most days. I like that you can set 2 alarms as well! This is nice for days I work vs. the weekend. Here is where the issues come in: I wish it was brighter. I sleep with the alarm set to 20 (highest setting) and next to my head, but it’s still not enough some mornings. The other issue is it arrived with a big crack above the “Select” button on the screen. I’m hoping to get some customer support and maybe a replacement soon? The crack seems to be getting bigger so I’m worried it will eventually cause more damage to the unit.

reviews.author: Timothy
reviews.title: Too easy to forget to set; doesn’t wake my heavy sleeper
reviews.content_html:
There’s not a permanent way to set the alarm. Because of the touch interface, it’s easy to inadvertently disable the alarm so it won’t wake you up the next day.

It’s a beautiful clock. I love the way it gradually increases the light intensity. Between the light and the sound It wakes me up and I’m in a room at the end of the hall.

My daughter is a heavy sleeper and she sleeps right through it.

reviews.content_html: This thing is ridiculously complicated to set. I followed the directions meticulously and in the morning the radio blared at me and no light came on. The soothing music is sharp and agitating.

reviews.author: Blauzen
reviews.title: Impossible set up with known firmware defect
reviews.content_html: Ridiculous set up! I'm an educated person and normally fairly adept at electronics and this thing is impossible. After fighting with it for an hour both using the included instructions, which were written by somebody who apparently has never used the item, and utilizing ChatGPT, I find out there's a firmware problem that Phillips knows about and has not fixed. I'm gonna send this piece of junk back and order another (because it is allegedly the best model in its class) in the hopes that I get a different batch that isn't defective. If I don't update this review with a much better experience, do not buy this thing !!!

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.title: Poor product. Not even worth it if given for free
reviews.content_html:
Works as expected for the sunrise feature. But thats it. There isnt a way to turn off the light after the alarm/sunrise is on. There isn't a way to set a custom snooze time. You only get 9 mins of snooze.

I wouldn't recommend. The design is horrible. Buttons are too small and awkwardly placed. The device is very light and you'll need to use two hands to press the buttons properly.

Instruction manual is poorly written. You're better off just pressing buttons and just learn as you go.

reviews.author: Scott
reviews.title: Garbage
reviews.content_html:
Absolute garbage. So confusing to set up! I read the instruction manual multiple times but then had to resort to google and Reddit to figure it out. I’m not an idiot, but this clock made me feel like one.

Next, even the loudest alarm setting isn’t nearly loud enough to wake up my teenage son. Further, the alarm sounds are all very pleasant, but again, because they are all so peasant they won’t wake up my son. I had hoped the combination of the sunrise effect and the alarm would do the trick, but this was a waste of time and money.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.title: 1990s design
reviews.content_html: Very Meh. For being $150 it’s amazing it doesn’t even have Bluetooth instead has FM radio. I assumed there was a ‘touch’ component to turn on/off the light…nope just a tiny analog button that’s among other buttons, so you have to mash several buttons before you get it. The layout in terms of setup is not intuitive. Seems extremely- old school, like it was designed and manufactured in the mid 1990s. Several very tiny hard to see/ feel buttons along the edge operate everything. The bulb however has a great variety of brightness and is exactly what I want. Unfortunately I’ll probably return it due UI layout, inability to customize and difficulty to balance.
